teamObjective.explanation: Because my hand is entirely low non-trump clubs and an opponent is near finish, the priority is to shed cards while conceding as little control as possible. Playing the 3 preserves my higher cards and gives partner the best chance to cover the trick. The marginal defensive gain of leading the 6 does not outweigh the loss of my only modestly stronger card.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner (player 2, 3 cards) has not acted; no direct signal exists. With three cards remaining, partner could be holding one or more mid-to-high cards that can cover a low lead, but I should not assert this as fact. Leading low invites partner to take the trick if they have a stronger single or to shed safely if they do not.

opponentBelief.summary: Opponent player 1 has only 2 cards and is therefore near finishing; they will likely try to win any cheap lead to retain control and exit. Opponent player 3 has 3 cards and is also a threat, but less imminent. Because no cards have been played, I cannot assume which ranks or suits they hold, only that they are incentivized to exploit a weak opening lead.

Playing the 3 preserves my higher cards and gives partner the best chance to cover the trick. The marginal defensive gain of leading the 6 does not outweigh the loss of my only modestly stronger card.
